fund.name: Aditya Birla Sun Life Medium Term Plan - Growth - Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Limited

allocations.rationale: Debt funds are safer and provide stable returns. They are suitable for a moderate risk appetite.
Gold is a safe investment and can provide good returns. It is suitable for a moderate risk appetite.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww, Kuvera, or MFU. Step 2: Start your monthly SIPs in these funds.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if your SIPs are running.
After 1 year: See if any fund has fallen behind compared to similar funds.
After 3 years: Rebalance your portfolio to maintain your target allocation.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check if your mix has changed a lot. If one category has grown much more than planned, move some money to the others to get back to your target mix.
plan.rationale: Since you're 52 with 13 years ahead, we can afford to put more in stocks which grow faster over long periods. We've kept 10% in safer options so your money isn't all in one basket.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will temporarily go down. This is normal and recovers over time.
Debt funds are safer but offer lower returns. Gold is a safe investment but can be volatile.
The market may not perform as expected, leading to lower returns than projected.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

aps.reasoning: The plan has a 0.0 passive_instrument_fraction because all equity allocations are to actively managed funds. The turnover_score is 0.3 due to annual rebalancing and some tactical shifts. The cost_emphasis_score is 0.2 as costs are not a primary focus. The research_vs_cost_score is 0.2 since the plan emphasizes fund rationale over cost. The time_horizon_alignment_score is 1.0 due to long-term orientation. The portfolio_activeness_score is 0.2 as the funds have high alpha and tracking error, indicating active management.

pqs.reasoning: The plan aligns well with the investor's moderate risk appetite and 13-year horizon, allocating 80% to equities. Diversification is moderate across equity categories but lacks significant exposure to hybrid funds or fixed income alternatives. The risk-return profile is appropriate for a moderate investor, though the projected returns may be optimistic. Internal consistency is good, with rationale supporting allocations. Tax efficiency is neutral as the plan doesn't explicitly address tax implications for a 30% slab investor.
